Question Text: Which of the following statements is NOT true regarding protostome diversity?
Option 1: Of the 30 animal phyla more than 2/3 are protostomes.
Option 2: There are more mollusk species than annelid species.
Option 3: There are more lophotrochozoans than ecdysozoans.
Option 4: Nearly all protostome species are insects.

Question Text: The protostome developmental sequence arose just once in evolutionary history resulting in two main subgroups—Lophotrochozoa and Ecdysozoa. What does this finding most likely suggest?
Option 1: The two subgroups have a common ancestor that was a deuterostome.
Option 2: The protostomes are a polyphyletic group.
Option 3: Division of these two groups occurred after the protostome developmental sequence appeared.

Question Text: The common ancestor of the protostomes had a coelom. What does this suggest?
Option 2: There are no pseudocoelomates within the protostomes.
Option 3: There are no acoelomates within the protostomes.
Option 4: The coelom evolved before the lophophore.

Question Text: A terrestrial animal species is discovered with the following larval characteristics: exoskeleton system of tubes for gas exchange and modified segmentation. A knowledgeable zoologist should predict that the adults of this species would also feature ___.

What would this imply about protostome evolution? 1. Segmentation evolved only once within protostomes. 2. The annelid lineage gained and then lost the ability to molt. 3. The annelid lineage gained and then lost jointed limbs.
The presence of a lophophore in a newly discovered species would suggest that the species _______.
Option 2: grows by shedding its external covering
Option 3: is more closely related to an arthropod than a mollusk
Option 4: is more closely related to an annelid than a roundworm

Question Text: Which one of the following is a lophotrochozoan?
You find what you believe is a new species of animal. Which of the following characteristics would enable you to argue that it is more closely related to a flatworm than it is to a roundworm?
Option 1: It has a cuticle that it sheds to grow.
Option 4: It has a mouth and an anus.

Question Text: Arthropods invaded land about 100 million years before vertebrates did so. This most clearly implies that _______.
Option 2: extant terrestrial arthropods are better adapted to terrestrial life than are extant terrestrial vertebrates
Option 3: ancestral arthropods must have been poorly adapted to aquatic life and thus experienced a selective pressure to invade land
Option 5: arthropods have had more time to coevolve with land plants than have vertebrates

Question Text: Adaptations to terrestrial environments evolved independently in arthropods ; mollusks ; nematodes ; and annelids. What logical conclusion would you make from this statement?
Option 1: All these groups have a coelom.
Option 2: The common ancestor of all these groups was probably aquatic.
Option 3: All these groups have the same type of body plan.
Option 4: All these groups lost their coelom after moving to land.

Upon close inspection of movement in annelids and nematodes you would notice an obvious difference. Nematodes tend to wriggle back and forth while annelids tend to contract along their length as their girth swells and then thin out as they stretch. What anatomical feature explains this type of movement?
Option 1: The annelids possess longitudinal muscle fibers that the nematodes lack.

Question Text: The heartworms that can accumulate within the hearts of dogs and other mammals have a pseudocoelom an alimentary canal and an outer covering that is occasionally shed. To which phylum does the heartworm belong?

A student observes a wormlike organism crawling about on dead organic matter. Later ; the organism sheds its outer covering. One possibility is that the organism is a larval insect (like a maggot) (P. arthropoda). However ; it could also be a member of the phylum _______ ; and one way to distinguish between the two possibilities is by looking for the presence or absence of _______.
Option 5: Annelida; muscle in the body wall

Question Text: Tardigrades differ from arthropods in that they _______.

Option 2: It has one pair of legs per segment.

Question Text:
Use the following information to answer the question(s) below.

Many terrestrial arthropods exchange gases with their environments by using tracheae, tubes that lead from openings (called spiracles) in the animal’s exoskeleton or cuticle directly to the animal’s tissues. Some arthropods can control whether their spiracles are open or closed; opening the spiracles allows carbon dioxide produced in the tissues to travel down the tracheae and be released outside the animal. Klok et al. measured the carbon dioxide emitted over time (represented by VCO?) by several species of centipedes. The figure below presents graphs of their results for two species, Cormocephalus morsitans and Scutigera weberi. (C. J. Klok, R. D. Mercer, and S. L. Chown. 2002. Discontinuous gas exchange in centipedes and its convergence with tracheated arthropods. Journal of Experimental Biology 205: 1019–29.) Copyright 2002 The Company of Biologists and the Journal of Experimental Biology.   Look at the graph for Cormocephalus morsitans in the accompanying figure. What is the best interpretation of these results?
Option 1: The centipede had its spiracles open the entire time.
Option 2: The centipede had its spiracles closed the entire time.
Option 3: The centipede had its spiracles open when carbon dioxide (CO2) emission peaked and closed when CO2 emission was low.
Option 4: The centipede had its spiracles closed when carbon dioxide (CO2) emission peaked and open when CO2 emission was low.

Question Text: How would a terrestrial centipede most likely benefit from the ability to close its spiracles? Closing spiracles would _______.
Option 1: allow the centipede to move more quickly
Option 2: allow the centipede to retain more moisture in its tissues
Option 3: allow the centipede to stay warmer
Option 4: allow more oxygen from the environment to reach the centipede’s tissues
Option 5: not benefit the centipede at all

Question Text: What hypothesis can you make about each centipede’s habitat?
Option 1: C. morsitans lives in a habitat that provides more carbon dioxide than does S. weberi.
Option 2: C. morsitans lives in a habitat with more predators than does S. weberi.
Option 3: C. morsitans lives in a colder habitat than does S. weberi.
Option 4: C. morsitans lives in a drier habitat than does S. weberi.

Question Text: The possession of two pairs of antennae is a characteristic of _______.

Whiteflies are common pest insects found on cotton ; tomato ; poinsettia ; and many other plants. Nymphs are translucent and mostly sessile ; feeding on their host plants’ phloem (sap) from the undersides of leaves. They undergo incomplete metamorphosis into winged adults. Because whitefly nymphs cannot escape predation by moving ; you hypothesize that their translucent bodies make them hard to spot by predators. How could you directly test this hypothesis?
Option 1: Compare rates of predation on whitefly nymphs on plant leaves of different colors.
Option 2: Compare rates of predation on whitefly
Option 3: Compare rates of predation on whitefly nymphs versus whitefly adults.
Option 4: Compare rates of predation on whitefly nymphs by predators that are translucent versus predators that are not translucent.
Option 5: Compare rates of predation on whitefly nymphs feeding on poisonous plants versus those feeding on nonpoisonous plants.

Question Text: Sarah noticed the presence of many empty exoskeletons attached to emergent vegetation. These exoskeletons looked exactly like those of the largest of the mystery organisms she had seen in the pond. They also looked similar to the bodies of the dragonflies that patrolled the surface of the pond. Option 1: They are larval dragonflies destined to undergo incomplete metamorphosis.

Option 3: They are adult dragonflies so old that they can no longer fly have fallen into the pond but have not yet drowned.
